title: xyris phaeocephala
intro: Xyris phaeocephala, also known as yellow-eyed grass, is a perennial plant species native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. Consuming Xyris phaeocephala has been associated with various health benefits, including reducing fever, alleviating respiratory issues, and supporting immune function. The plant is typically prepared as a tea or tincture, and it can also be used in topical applications for skin conditions and wounds.
